description: A deterministic tempo-based turn RPG CLI that simulates battles based on a fixed ruleset and reproduces golden results for 4 scenarios.

files.role: entry point: handles CLI arguments, initializes scenarios, and prints battle results

interfaces.name: main
interfaces.description: Parses --scenario argument, creates the Entity list, runs the BattleEngine, and prints final state.

files.role: data contracts: defines the core state objects

interfaces.name: Entity
interfaces.description: id:str, name:str, team:str, max_hp:int, hp:int, atk:int, defense:int, spd:int, gauge:float, statuses:list, last_skill:str|None, skills:list, skill_idx:int

files.role: logic for status application, interaction matrix, and periodic effects

interfaces.name: apply_status
interfaces.signature: def apply_status(target: Entity, s_type: str, turns: int, stacks: int = 1) -> None
interfaces.description: Applies a status effect to a target while handling the interaction matrix (Burn/Freeze/Shock/Poison).

interfaces.name: process_tick_start
interfaces.description: Handles turn-start effects like Burn damage.

interfaces.name: process_tick_end
interfaces.description: Handles turn-end effects like Poison damage and status duration decay.

interfaces.name: calculate_damage
interfaces.signature: def calculate_damage(actor: Entity, target: Entity, base: int) -> int
interfaces.description: Implements max(1, atk + base - def) with the 1.25x Shock multiplier (floored).

interfaces.name: execute_skill
interfaces.signature: def execute_skill(actor: Entity, target: Entity, skill_name: str) -> None
interfaces.description: Processes the specific effect of a skill, including combos and status application.

files.role: tempo turn simulation, AI targeting, and battle loop

interfaces.name: BattleEngine
interfaces.description: Manages the battle loop, gauge increments, priority selection, and victory conditions.

interfaces.name: run
interfaces.description: Executes the battle until a team is wiped or turns exceed 100. Returns winner, turns, and final hps.

key_points: Strict adherence to the damage formula: max(1, atk + base - target.defense) * 1.25 (floor) if shock exists
Deterministic tempo turn priority: spd (desc) -> hp (asc) -> registration order
Last-skill combo chain reset by any actual damage received or freeze skip
Status interaction matrix: Burn/Freeze override/removal and Shock/Poison stack multiplier
HP floor at 0: No negative HP, specifically 0 upon death
Turn count increments per action attempt (including freezes)

requirements.text: Implement a damage formula with 1.25x shock multiplier and a minimum of 1 damage
Handle status effects: Burn (start), Poison (end), Freeze (skip), Shock (dmg boost)
Implement the status interaction matrix (Burn+Freeze, Shock+Poison)
Support skill combos (ignite -> detonate, charge -> combo_strike)
Tempo-turn system based on spd and gauge (100 threshold), with specific priority rules
AI targeting based on lowest HP of enemies
Exact output format for winner, total turns, and per-entity HP
Reproduce golden results for 4 specific scenarios deterministically
Use only Python standard library, no input() or eval()

acceptance_criteria: The simulation logic precisely calculates damage, status effects, and turn priority as defined in the rules
The tempo turn loop correctly handles tick-start, action (or freeze), and tick-end sequences
Running the CLI for scenarios 1-4 produces the exact golden winner, turn count, and entity HP values

criteria_checks.criterion: The simulation logic precisely calculates damage, status effects, and turn priority as defined in the rules
The tempo turn loop correctly handles tick-start, action (or freeze), and tick-end sequences
Running the CLI for scenarios 1-4 produces the exact golden winner, turn count, and entity HP values

success_signal.command: python main.py --scenario 1 && python main.py --scenario 2 && python main.py --scenario 3 && python main.py --scenario 4
